title: SQL Data Management
subtitle: Learn to insert, update, delete, and create tables safely in SQL.
learningGoals: Insert rows using explicit column lists.
Insert multiple rows safely.
Understand defaults, NULL values, and required columns.
Preview rows before changing data.
Update selected rows using WHERE.
Delete selected rows using WHERE.
Explain why unsafe UPDATE and DELETE statements are dangerous.
Create beginner tables with clear columns.
Choose simple SQLite-friendly data types.
Use beginner constraints such as PRIMARY KEY, NOT NULL, UNIQUE, and FOREIGN KEY.
Validate schema changes with follow-up queries.

modules.title: Inserting Data
modules.purpose: Shift from reading data to adding new rows carefully and correctly.

topics.title: What INSERT INTO Does
topics.summary: Understand what INSERT INTO does so you can explain the idea in beginner-friendly language.
learningGoals: Describe what INSERT INTO does in plain language.
Identify a beginner SQL scenario that depends on understanding what INSERT INTO does.

topics.title: Insert One Row
topics.summary: Practice insert one row so you can use it in a simple SQL query or workflow.
learningGoals: Use Insert One Row in a simple SQL query or workflow.
Explain what result or output insert one row should produce.
